data.title: Bitwise CEO Discusses Shifts in Cryptocurrency Market Dynamics
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley recently commented on the evolving nature of the cryptocurrency market. Horsley noted that while discussions often revolve around a four-year cycle, this model is rooted in the past era of cryptocurrencies. 

He highlighted that since the introduction of Bitcoin ETFs and the appointment of new management, the market structure has undergone significant changes. These include the emergence of new participants, dynamics, and motivations for buying and selling. Horsley expressed the belief that the market may have experienced a bear phase lasting nearly six months and is on the verge of recovery. 

He concluded by stating that the current environment for cryptocurrency development is more favorable than ever before.

data.title: Binance will delist the COIN-M RUNEUSD perpetual contract on November 19, 2025.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance Futures will automatically close and settle the COIN-MRUNEUSD perpetual contract at 09:00 (UTC) on November 19, 2025, and will delist the contract after completion. From 08:30 (UTC) on November 19, 2025, users will be unable to open new positions. It is recommended to proactively close positions before the contract is delisted to avoid automatic settlement. (This news item was generated with AI assistance.)

data.description:

data.title: U.S. Economic Outlook: Liquidity Surge Anticipated Post-Government Reopening
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Raoul Pal, co-founder and CEO of Real Vision, has shared insights on the macroeconomic trajectory following the reopening of the U.S. government. He predicts that in the coming days, the U.S. Treasury General Account (TGA) will significantly boost market liquidity, a trend expected to persist for several months. Quantitative tightening (QT) is anticipated to conclude in December, with the balance sheet beginning a gradual increase. Consequently, the U.S. dollar is likely to weaken again.

Pal suggests that policymakers' next critical move is to prevent a year-end liquidity crunch. He foresees the introduction of several "temporary" measures to enhance liquidity, with regular financing and the Standing Repo Facility (SRF) operations being the most probable. Debt must be rolled over, and the government aims to stimulate the economy ahead of the midterm elections. Overall, a wave of liquidity is expected to flood the market.

data.title: Whale '7 Siblings' Acquires Significant Ethereum Holdings
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the whale known as '7 Siblings' has made a substantial investment in Ethereum. On November 14, Onchain Lens reported that '7 Siblings' purchased 2,211 ETH at an average price of $3,166, totaling $7 million USDS.

Since November 4, the whale has acquired a total of 44,008 ETH, valued at approximately $152.99 million, with an average purchase price of $3,477.

data.title: Big whale 7Siblings bought 2211 ETH at an average price of $3166.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, the whale "7 Siblings" recently invested 7 million USDS to buy 2,211 ETH at an average price of $3,166. Since November 4, the whale has accumulated 44,008 ETH, with a total value of approximately $152.99 million and an average purchase cost of approximately $3,477.

data.title: CZ: Every time there's a downturn, some people think it's the "end of an era," but time always moves forward.
data.description: CZ posted on the X platform that whenever the market corrects, some people always think that "the era is over," but time always moves forward.

data.title: OKX launches SEIUSDT perpetual contracts, supporting up to 50x leverage.
data.description: OKX announced that it officially launched the SEIUSDT perpetual contract on its website, app, and API at 15:30 (UTC+8) on November 14, 2025. The contract is based on the SEI/USDT index, settles in USDT, has a face value of 10, offers a maximum leverage of 50x, and has a funding fee range of -1.50% to 1.50%, settled every 4 hours. According to the announcement, if the funding fee reaches its upper or lower limit, the system will automatically adjust the settlement frequency to once per hour. The contract is available 24/7.

data.title: South Korea to increase EV subsidies to counter Trump tariffs in 2026
data.description:
The South Korean government has announced plans to increase EV subsidies by 20% in 2026 in response to the ongoing effects of U. S. tariffs.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/south-korea-raise-2026-ev-subsidies/

data.title: Ethereum Developers Report Progress on Testnet Fork
data.description: According to Foresight News, Christine Kim, Vice President of Research at Galaxy, summarized the 169th Ethereum Core Developers Consensus (ACDC) call. This week, developers reported smooth progress on the second block parameter-only (BPO) hard fork initiated on the Hoodi testnet. Client teams are nearing completion of interoperability test preparations, with Teku's ePBS implementation being the closest to running on Kurtosis. Ethereum advocate and ETH investor Ryan Berckmans opposed the inclusion of a forced inclusion list (FOCIL) in the Glamsterdam merge.

data.title: "Brother Machi" has accumulated losses of $15.73 million over the past 35 days, and his current long position with a leverage of 25 times is showing a floating loss of $2.27 million.
data.description: Crypto analyst Ai (@ai_9684xtpa) monitors data showing that in the 35 days since the market crash on October 11th, Machi made 18 trades on Hyperliquid, with 5 profitable trades and 13 losing trades, a win rate of 27.7%, all of which were long positions. During this period, her cumulative losses reached $15.73 million, and she added $6.102 million in margin. Currently, Machi's account has approximately $946,000 in remaining margin, holding 7,500.7 ETH long positions with 25x leverage, a position size of approximately $23.68 million, and a current unrealized loss of approximately $2.27 million. The liquidation price is $3,043.16.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Machines Face Shutdown Price Challenges
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Antpool indicates that several Bitcoin mining machines, including the Antminer S19 series and models below the Whatsminer M60 series, such as the Whatsminer M53S++ and M56S++, have reached their shutdown price. This calculation is based on the current Bitcoin mining difficulty and an electricity cost of $0.08 per kilowatt-hour.

The Whatsminer M60 and M66 models are nearing a shutdown price of $93,000. In contrast, high-performance mining machines like the Antminer U3S23H and Antminer S23 Hyd have a shutdown price above $44,000.

data.title: Antminer S19 and other models below Shenma M60 have reached the shutdown price.
data.description: According to Antpool data, under current Bitcoin mining difficulty and an electricity cost of $0.08 per kilowatt-hour, the Antminer S19 series and Whatsminer models below the M60 (including M53S++ and M56S++) have reached their shutdown price; while the shutdown price of the Whatsminer M60 and M66 series is close to $93,000. Meanwhile, high-hashrate models such as the Antminer U3S23H and Antminer S23 Hyd have shutdown prices above $44,000.

data.title: Bitcoin Investors' Average Cost Suggests Uncertain Bear Market Cycle
data.description: According to Odaily, Ki Young Ju, founder and CEO of CryptoQuant, shared insights on the X platform regarding Bitcoin investments. He noted that the average cost for Bitcoin investors who entered the market in the past 6 to 12 months is approximately $94,000. Ju emphasized that a bear market cycle can only be confirmed if prices fall below this cost range. He advised against drawing premature conclusions and suggested maintaining a cautious stance.

data.title: Bitwise CEO: The crypto market may have been in a bear market for nearly six months and is nearing its end.
data.description: Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley stated in an article on the X platform that the traditional "four-year crypto cycle" model is no longer applicable to the current market structure. Since the launch of the Bitcoin ETF and changes in the regulatory environment, market participants and trading motivations have undergone significant shifts. He noted that while the crypto market may have been in a bear market for the past six months, it is now nearing the end of the downturn and believes that the overall allocation environment for crypto assets has "never been stronger."
